# Evidence index

<!-- Generated by mlx-model-porting/scripts/generate_evidence_index.py; edit sources.yaml, not this file. -->

**Snapshot date:** 2026-07-12
**Total records:** 356

This is the deterministic, navigable rendering of the canonical `mlx-model-porting/assets/sources.yaml` registry. The registry order is author-curated; this view sorts records by ID without rewriting that order.

Local workload receipts and their limitations are evaluated in the [Benchmark assessment](mlx-model-porting/assets/BENCHMARK_REPORT.md). A catalogue citation or source-reported number does not prove target-workload performance.

## Evidence semantics

### Review depth

Review depth states how far a source was reviewed and used by this project. It is not an MLX reproduction status.

| Review depth | Records | Meaning |
|---|---:|---|
| `indexed` | 24 | Catalogued for future review; do not represent as fully reviewed. |
| `screened` | 190 | Abstract/readme/code path reviewed for relevance and limits. |
| `synthesized` | 142 | Directly informed a runbook, rule, or technique decision. |

### Support scope

Support scope states whose implementation or receipt the evidence covers. It never widens a source beyond its recorded snapshot.

| Support scope | Records | Meaning |
|---|---:|---|
| `context_only` | 1 | Context, discovery, or source-selection evidence. It does not establish implementation support or performance. |
| `local_reproduced` | 0 | Local benchmark or validation evidence. It applies only to the captured hardware, software, model, inputs, and workload. |
| `not-classified` | 326 | No support scope is encoded. The record is citation evidence only and grants no implicit support claim. |
| `official_mlx` | 14 | Apple-maintained MLX framework evidence. It establishes source or API support at the recorded snapshot, not target-workload performance. |
| `official_mlx_project` | 4 | Apple-maintained project evidence built on MLX. It establishes project-scoped behavior, not a framework-wide guarantee. |
| `paper_only` | 1 | Algorithm or architecture evidence without a demonstrated MLX implementation. Reproduce the path and pass parity before recommending it. |
| `third_party_pinned` | 10 | Pinned non-Apple MLX implementation evidence. Treat it as reproducible prior art, not an official framework guarantee. |

### Claim boundary

Every source is constrained by the following claim-boundary rules:

- Review depth describes how the source informed this skill; it is not proof that the source was reproduced on MLX.
- A source without evidence_class, support_scope, or claim_types is catalogue evidence only and grants no implicit implementation, performance, memory, or quality claim.
- Performance, memory, quality, and audio-quality claims stay attached to the exact recorded hardware, software, model, inputs, workload, and metric.
- A record note may narrow these boundaries. The narrowest applicable boundary always wins.

Claim types describe what a classified record may support; absent claim types grant no implicit claim.

| Claim type | Records | Meaning |
|---|---:|---|
| `algorithm_candidate` | 0 | The source supports considering an algorithm, not claiming that the repository implements it. |
| `api_support` | 15 | The cited API exists at the recorded snapshot; compatibility with a target model still requires validation. |
| `audio_quality` | 1 | An audio-quality claim requires the cited listening or objective evaluation conditions. |
| `memory` | 1 | A memory claim is limited to the exact measured setup and accounting method. |
| `mlx_implementation` | 15 | An MLX implementation exists in the cited scope; this does not by itself prove parity or speed on another target. |
| `performance` | 3 | A latency or throughput claim is limited to the exact measured setup and must not be generalized. |
| `quality` | 0 | A quality claim is limited to the recorded task, dataset, metric, and configuration. |
| `risk_or_negative` | 2 | The source records a failure, limitation, or risk; it is not positive support for the technique. |
| `serving_semantics` | 7 | The source supports stated scheduling, caching, or isolation behavior only in the cited implementation. |
